name: Arched bridge (stone span between two points)
description: A stone arched bridge spanning a moat, river, or ravine between two abutments. A flat @primary walking surface 2-3 blocks wide runs along the longer horizontal axis of the AABB at the top of the envelope. Below it, 1-2 arched soffits are formed from @stairs blocks (half=top, facing inward) on each long edge, rising from the abutments toward a flat @primary apex band — the arch's keystone course. Two support pillars of @secondary at each end (2-3 tall) carry the abutment thrust under the deck. Railings of @fence line both long edges of the deck one block above the surface, with 2-4 minecraft:lantern posts at evenly spaced intervals replacing fence cells (always including the two ends, plus 1-2 mid-points on longer spans). Optional @stairs ramps at the ends lead up to the deck on bridges long enough to make sense of them. On spans long enough to host two arches (>= 12), a central @secondary pier connects the apex down to the ground.

required_furniture.rule: Fill of @primary at y_deck = y1 - 1 across the full span, 2-3 cells wide along the short axis (centred).

1-2 arches built from @stairs[half=top] on the long-edge flanks rising toward the centre, plus a flat @primary apex band one row below the deck. Each arch has stair limbs of length min(arch_depth, (arch_span - 1) // 2) on each side; arch_depth is 1 for height < 5 and 2 otherwise. Double-arch spans (span_len >= 12) split the bridge with a 1-cell gap hosting the central pier.

Two abutment pillars of @secondary at the bridge ends (u = u0 and u = u1 - 1), 2 blocks tall on short bridges and 3 on tall ones; mirrored across the deck-width cells so both deck columns rest on stone.
Two rows of @fence one block above the deck (y_rail = y_deck + 1) on both long edges of the walking surface, running the full span length.
minecraft:lantern blocks replace fence cells at evenly spaced u positions on BOTH rails — 2 lanterns (each end) for span_len < 10, 3 for 10..13, 4 for >= 14. Always include u0 and u1 - 1.
Optional @stairs[half=bottom] facing inward at each abutment cell (u0 and u1 - 1) one row below the deck; placed only when span_len >= 6 and the abutment pillars don't already fill the ramp row.

placement_rules: Pillars at ends support the structure
Bridge length runs along max(W, D); width is clamped to the short axis (2-3 deck cells, centred)
Lanterns sit on the railings and always include both ends so the bridge mouth reads as illuminated
Double-arch bridges (span_len >= 12) have a central @secondary pier between the two arches
Defensive on AABBs from 3x4x6 (small) up to 5x6x16 (long); out-of-range inputs clamp to this envelope
lighting_ventilation: Lanterns at the railing intervals illuminate the deck at night; the open spans under the arches let water, mist, and breeze pass freely so the bridge does not dam the channel beneath it. As an exterior structure ventilation is implicit — the deck is open-air on top and beneath the soffit only the apex band is solid.

medieval.notes: Cobblestone deck and arches over stone-brick abutments with oak-plank walking surface optional; classic castle moat bridge.

modern.notes: Smooth-stone deck and arches over polished-andesite piers with iron-bar railings — civic infrastructure rather than fortification.

fantasy.notes: Mossy cobblestone arches with dark-oak fence railings — a weathered, overgrown crossing fit for forest temples or ruined keeps.
